links.targetTitle: Live collaboration's only UI surface is the document toolbar History view, not per-editor cursors.

global.title: Collaborate tab in chat panel is the live-collab UX entry point
global.content:
## UX entry point

Live collaboration is surfaced through a **Collaborate tab in the chat panel**, not through any editor chrome.

## Flow

1. Pick a **drive or doc** as the collaboration target.
2. **Multi-select participants by Swarm ID**, reusing the document-share picker pattern.
3. Invitation is delivered as a **chat card** with `[Join]` / `[Decline]` buttons.
4. An **active collabs list** shows participant avatars and a last-activity timestamp.

## Explicit non-goal

**No editor-level UI work.** No cursor overlays, no caret labels, no per-editor presence widgets. The toolbar's existing History view -- auto-refreshed when a GSOC `op-committed` ping debounces in -- is the entire presence surface.

## Why this shape

Reusing the document-share picker keeps the mental model consistent: collaboration is a natural extension of sharing, addressed by Swarm ID, negotiated via a chat card. It avoids building per-editor UI for a set of editors (tables, visual canvases, dashboards, button grids) where no shared cursor metaphor would generalize.
global.description: Live collab UX reuses the document-share picker pattern: Collaborate tab to pick drive/doc, multi-select participants, send invitation card with Join/Decline. No editor UI.
